text.title: A/B Testing Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the ab-testing-basics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Hypothesis, variants, primary metric, MDE, and target sample size are written down BEFORE the experiment launches. No mid-flight changes to the primary metric.

rationale.source: Kohavi et al — Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments

rule.rationale: Mid-flight metric changes are p-hacking; pre-registration is the only defence against motivated reasoning.

rule.statement: Bucket users by hash(experiment_id + user_id) % 10000 (or similar). Never use timestamps, random per-request, or order of arrival.
rule.rationale: Random per-request assignment causes the same user to see different variants across sessions, contaminating conversion measurement.

rule.statement: Each experiment has exactly one primary metric. Secondary metrics are tracked but never used to declare a winner.
rule.rationale: Multiple primary metrics inflate false-positive rate; the analyst will always find one that 'won'.

rule.statement: Compute the target sample size from baseline rate + MDE + power (0.8) + alpha (0.05) BEFORE launch.
rule.rationale: Launching without a sample-size target leads to peeking; the test runs until it 'wins'.

rule.statement: Do not call winners before the target sample size is reached unless using a sequential testing framework that controls Type-I error.
rule.rationale: Peeking inflates false-positive rates 3–5×; sequential testing is allowed but requires explicit alpha-spending.

rule.statement: Ramp traffic 1% → 5% → 25% → 50%; abort on guardrail metric regression (latency, error rate) ≥10%.
rule.rationale: Ramping bounds the blast radius if treatment is broken; guardrails catch silent regressions outside the primary metric.

rule.statement: If there is no clear primary metric, no instrumentation, or sample size is insufficient for 80% power, skip this methodology and instrument first.
rule.rationale: Running an underpowered experiment wastes traffic and produces noise that looks like signal.
